tasks.title: Set up design system — dark chat theme with semantic tokens
tasks.goal: Create a polished dark/light design system with semantic tokens for the chatbot UI
acceptance_criteria: Semantic color tokens for chat bubbles (user vs bot), background, foreground, muted, borders
Typography scale defined for chat messages, headings, captions
Dark mode support with proper token switching
Custom shadows and border-radius for chat bubble aesthetic

tasks.title: Build chat UI — message list, input bar, and typing indicator
tasks.goal: Create the full chat interface with message bubbles, input area, and bot typing animation
acceptance_criteria: Chat window with scrollable message list
User messages aligned right, bot messages aligned left with distinct styling
Input bar with send button, supports Enter to send
Animated typing indicator (3 bouncing dots) while bot responds
Auto-scroll to latest message
Welcome message from bot on first load
Responsive — works on mobile and desktop
All colors use semantic tokens — no hardcoded colors
Smooth transitions on all interactive elements

tasks.title: Build AI service — OpenAnalyst API integration with PM-only system prompt
tasks.goal: Create the API service that calls OpenAnalyst with a strict product management system prompt
acceptance_criteria: System prompt restricts bot to product management topics only
Bot politely declines non-PM questions
Uses OpenAnalyst API at https://api.openanalyst.com/api
Handles errors gracefully with user-friendly messages
Supports streaming or non-streaming response

tasks.title: Build embeddable widget mode — floating chat button + popup
tasks.goal: Create a widget version that can be embedded on any website via a script tag
acceptance_criteria: Floating chat button in bottom-right corner
Click opens chat popup with smooth animation
Close button to minimize back to floating button
Widget can be embedded via a single script tag
Example embed HTML file provided
Widget works independently on any website
Responsive — adapts size on mobile vs desktop

tasks.title: Fix AI response streaming — fast word-by-word reveal like Claude web
tasks.goal: Replace non-streaming API call + slow typewriter with real SSE streaming that delivers text chunk-by-chunk as the API produces it
acceptance_criteria: AI responses appear word-by-word as the API streams them
Response text appears at Claude-web-like speed (real-time, not 18ms/char)
Falls back gracefully if streaming fails
All existing functionality preserved (file upload, chat history, etc.)

tasks.title: Polish thinking/generating indicator — clear visual states during AI response
tasks.goal: Replace bouncing dots with Sparkles icon + shimmer animation + 'Thinking...' text, blinking cursor during streaming
acceptance_criteria: Thinking indicator shows with shimmer animation while waiting for first chunk
Blinking cursor appears at end of message while still streaming
Smooth transitions on all state changes
All colors use semantic tokens

tasks.title: Polish WelcomeView — gradient text, quick action micro-interactions, input glow
tasks.goal: Add visual polish to WelcomeView: gradient greeting text, floating icon animation, input focus glow, quick action hover lift with icon color change, ambient background glow, stronger send button press feedback
acceptance_criteria: Greeting has gradient text on user name/role
Sparkles icon uses float animation
Quick action pills lift on hover with shadow and border color change
Quick action pill icons change color on hover via group-hover
Input box gets glow shadow on focus
Subtle radial gradient glow behind greeting
Send button has active:scale-[0.92] for stronger press feedback
All colors use semantic tokens
Stagger entrance animations work

tasks.title: Add active files indicator + polish file chips with type-specific colors
tasks.goal: Add type-specific color coding to FileChip (green for CSV/Excel, red for PDF, blue for text/MD) and a collapsible active files indicator bar in ConversationView
acceptance_criteria: CSV/Excel file chips have green-tinted icon and left border accent
PDF file chips have red-tinted icon and left border accent
Text/MD file chips have blue-tinted icon and left border accent
Active files bar appears in conversation view when files are loaded
Active files bar shows file count, expands to show file names on click
Active files bar is collapsible (collapsed by default)
All colors use semantic tokens
Chips have hover effects and transitions
